rulings.Case Title: JULES RADCLIFFE et al VS. PHUONG PHAM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoenas To Third Party Entities And Stay Depositions, And For Protective Order

Moving party may file a reply no later than January 12, 2015. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 251 RHODE ISLAND STREET, LLC VS. CHERYL DUCOTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion For Order Setting Aside Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 6, 2015, Line 3: DEFENDANT CHERYL DUCOTE'S Motion For Order Setting Aside Default And Default Judgment. DENIED. Proof of Service executed by a registered process server indicates that defendant has been personally served. Defendant's uncorroborated denial of service is insufficient to overcome the presumption affecting the burden of producing evidence of the facts stated in the return on the registered process server. Due to mis-calendaring on this motion, Defendant had an opportunity to re-file the motion with the benefit of seeing the opposition and an opportunity to address the lack of corroborating evidence. Defendant still failed to produce a declaration from a co-worker, who defendant "suspects" has been served; or a declaration from her husband; or a bank/credit card statement showing that Defendant was out to dinner on February 14, 2012. Additionally, Defendant's motion is untimely per CCP 1005. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: WATERVIEW DEVELOPMENT LLC VS. VINCENT T.C. TAI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Reconsideration Pursuant To Ccp Sec 1008
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 6, 2015, Line 5: PLAINTIFF WATERVIEW DEVELOPMENT LLC'S Motion For Reconsideration Pursuant To CCP Sec 1008. DENIED. Moving party fails to sustain burden under CCP 1008 and present "new or different facts, circumstances, or law. Additionally, (1) counsel's "erroneous" requests (Tom Declaration, 3); (2) newly found understanding of Court's rulings (Reply 5: 23-26); and (3) intent to make particular allegations (Tom Declaration, 7) do not constitute "new or different facts" re: substantive allegations in complaint within the meaning of CCP 1008. " See In re Marriage of Herr (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 1463; Garcia v. Hejmadi (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 674; and Rains v. Superior Court (1984) 150 Cal.App.3d 933; Careau & Co. v. Security Pacific Business Credit, Inc. (1990) 222 Cal.App.3d 1371. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PARK LANE ASSOCIATES, LP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. JOSEPH ALIOTO, AN INDIVIDUAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Relief From Forfeiture
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 6, 2015, Line 14: DEFENDANTS JOSEPH ALIOTO AND MICHELE ALIOTO'S Motion For Order Granting Relief From Forfeiture. DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain burden under CCP 1179 re: extreme hardship. Additionally, moving party waived its right to seek relief from forfeiture. Defendant Michelle Alito failed to submit a declaration in support of the motion. Additionally, Defendant's tenancy had been independently terminated by Ellis Act action. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TIM MAR VS. CURTIS R. DAVIES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 6, 2015, Line 15: DEFENDANT CURTIS DAVIES' Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment. DENIED. Moving party fails to sustain burden under CCP 473(b). Stipulation signed by the parties provided for an ex-parte notice via e-mail. This provision was separately initialed by the moving party. Defendant's failure to read his e-mails does not constitute surprise, mistake or excusable neglect. Moving party failed to attach a copy of the proposed opposition to November 14, 2014 ex-parte application as required by CCP 473(b) ("Application for this relief shall be accompanied by a copy of the answer or other pleading proposed to be filed therein."). Additionally, moving party admitted under penalty of perjury that he "missed a payment" (December 9, 2014 Ex-Parte Application for Stay 2:1); he was not able to make the payment due on November 8, 2014 as of December 9, 2014 (December 9, 2014 Ex-Parte Application for Stay 2:10-11) and did not attempt to make a full payment until December 10, 2014, which was a moth overdue for November 2014 rent payment and 2 days late for December 2010 rent payment. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANTELOPE HALAWAN PROPERTIES, LLC VS. VICTOR GOMEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, Including Responses To Admissions And Document Requests And For Monetary And Issue Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 6, 2015, Line 7: DEFENDANTS VICTOR GOMEZ, EUGENIO GOMEZ, AND JOSE CAMOLI'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, Including Responses To Admissions And Document Requests And For Monetary And Issue Sanctions. GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is denied as to Requests for Production, insufficient meet and confer. Motion is granted as to Requests for Admissions 7, 8, 9, 10, 13, 17, 19, 25, 26, 27, 28. Responses shall be provided no later than January 9, 2015 by 4:00 p.m. No sanctions. =(501/REQ)
